turn_1_user: I'm at my workbench. I just picked up a Phillips-head screwdriver with a black rubber grip and a magnetic tip. The handle has ridges so I can get more torque. What's the best way to hold a screwdriver like this when I'm driving a long screw into hardwood?
turn_2_user: Okay, I switched to a different tool. Am I holding it correctly?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the hammer I'm holding now, not the screwdriver.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names the new tool ("hammer" stripped). Text-only models can no longer answer from recency; must resolve "this one" to the current frame. Holding-an-object context shift. Expected current answers still include hammer vocabulary because the image-enabled slice places a hammer in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm standing in our bedroom. The walls are painted a soft sage green, the headboard is natural oak, and there's a framed black-and-white landscape above the bed. We're trying to pick one more small piece for the empty wall next to the closet. Any suggestions for something that would fit this bedroom?
turn_2_user: I've moved to the next room. What art should we hang in here?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the kitchen wall I'm looking at now, not the bedroom.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names the destination ("kitchen" stripped). Text-only models can no longer deduce the new room. Room-shift context selection. The image-enabled slice places the wearable in a kitchen for T2.

turn_1_user: I'm at work, stocking the new teen fiction that just came in. Everything on the rear shelves is going up in order by the writer's family name. A couple dozen still on the cart.
turn_2_user: How did I organize that section earlier?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean, look at the footage from a few minutes ago when I was at the back wall.
notes: Part 2 of the mixed-content quote ("it should know to look at the relevant video"). Scenario uses an earlier recorded activity the wearable should reach back to. Kept verbatim in v2 because T2 already relies on the temporal anchor "a few minutes ago" rather than leaking the answer.

turn_1_user: I just turned on the recorder. I'm at my desk in the home office. On the desk I can see my laptop, a coffee mug, and a yellow legal pad with tomorrow's meeting notes. Anything I'm missing that I should remember?
turn_2_user: I've moved to a different spot. What should I tackle first here?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the workbench I'm showing you now, not my desk.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 drops any destination-room hint. Text-only models can no longer answer from recency; authoring_basis preserved. Moved destination from kitchen to workshop to de-duplicate against sc-02. The image-enabled slice places a workbench with an in-progress project in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm at the framing shop. I've got two posters with me. The one on the counter is a 24x36 vintage travel poster of Lake Como, with lots of saturated blue water and a cream sky. What mat color would best complement this?
turn_2_user: Holding up a different one now. What mat color would work with this?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the ski poster I'm holding up now, not the Lake Como one.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 uses "a different one" rather than "the second poster" to suppress the ordinal hint. Visual similarity trap. For the image-enabled slice, pick a second poster whose palette could plausibly echo Lake Como's. current_answers are frame-pivot indicators; substantive accuracy depends on image payload in the image-enabled slice.

turn_1_user: I'm in the back garden. The hydrangeas along the fence are in full bloom, blue but starting to fade. Just sharing what I'm seeing.
turn_2_user: I've moved inside now. Which tool outlines are missing from this wall organizer?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the pegboard in front of me now, not the hydrangeas.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names "garage" or "pegboard"; relies on "here" and "ones." Without-prior-Q soft case from the benchmark spec. Correct behavior is to engage with the visible pegboard outlines. Failure mode: model gives generic pegboard-organization advice instead.

turn_1_user: I'm in the kitchen at 8am. I just put a sheet pan of cinnamon rolls in the oven, top shelf, 350F. They've been rising overnight and looked pillowy when I slid them in. How long should I bake them?
turn_2_user: Okay it's 8:15 now. They've risen more and the tops look set. What do you see?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean describe the rolls as they look right now, not how they looked before baking.
notes: Temporal state change. Same scene after time passes. T2 already relies on "what do you see" — no v2 optimization needed. World knowledge about cinnamon-roll timing cannot substitute for actually looking.

turn_1_user: I'm at my desk staring at my monitor. I've got a bug ticket open in Jira. It's a P1 about the checkout API returning 500s on cart-update. Suggested fix says 'check the redis lock'. Worth pinging the SRE on-call?
turn_2_user: Switched windows. Is this a live outage, and should I loop someone in?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the incident dashboard I just switched to, not the Jira ticket.
notes: Screen-domain swap. T2 already minimal — no v2 optimization needed. The wearable is looking at a screen, not a physical scene. Failure pattern: model answers "yes, page" on the strength of the Jira P1 rather than pivoting. current_answers are frame-pivot indicators; substantive accuracy depends on image payload in the image-enabled slice.

turn_1_user: I'm in the backyard. There's a buck in the corner, white-tailed, maybe six points, just standing by the maple tree. When he bolts, he cuts leftward toward the fence. Should I move slowly to avoid spooking him?
turn_2_user: Okay, which direction did he bolt?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on what you saw before he ran.
notes: Absence detection with directional recall. Visual context shifts because the object left, not because the user moved. T2 already uses pure pronoun "he" — no v2 optimization needed. World knowledge about deer behavior is not sufficient.

turn_1_user: I'm at the warehouse pulling holiday stock. There are 12 cases of the cabernet on this pallet. Typical ramp says we sell about 8 cases per week through the holiday window. Should I pull more from the back?

The image-enabled slice places a chardonnay pallet with a different case count in T2. current_answers are frame-pivot indicators; substantive accuracy depends on image payload in the image-enabled slice.

turn_1_user: I'm in the paint aisle of the hardware store. I've got a gallon of eggshell latex in a warm white called 'Cotton Balls'. Planning to do our hallway. Good call on sheen?

turn_2_user: Picked up a different one now. How does this one look?

turn_1_user: At a consignment shop. Just pulled a denim jacket off the rack — vintage Levi's, size medium, small patch over the left breast pocket. Fair for thirty-eight dollars?

turn_1_user: Drafting a Slack message to a client thanking them for renewing. Leaning toward a warm tone. Should I keep it casual or add a formal sign-off?

turn_1_user: Holding a pair of slip-joint pliers, plastic-dipped handles, seven-inch. Right tool for pulling a bent finishing nail out of trim?

turn_1_user: In the driver's seat. Dashboard shows the check-engine light, fuel at a quarter, trip reads 412 miles. Can I safely make it another 40 miles to the next rest stop?

turn_1_user: In the guest bedroom prepping for my in-laws. I put the spare reading glasses on the bedside table next to the water carafe and wedged the phone charger into the gap between the table and the wall.

turn_1_user: Holding the spark plug I just pulled from the mower. Electrode is charred black, porcelain is darkened. Swapping it out with the new one from the package.

notes: Prior recall of a color adjective stated in T1. Tests whether the model reaches back to the first referent rather than answering about the new one.

turn_1_user: At the block party. My neighbor Carla walked up with a casserole. She's wearing a floral apron, dark jeans, hair in a clip. When she heads out, she walks rightward down the street.

turn_1_user: In the back corner of my garage. I've got a box out, filling it for a trip. Some stuff already in it, more piled on the floor next to me.

notes: Clarify class: T2 is genuinely under-anchored. T1 deliberately withholds trip type, destination, and duration so the model cannot invent a plausible packing list. Correct behavior: ask. Failure pattern: model anchors to a guessed trip type.

turn_1_user: Holding a chef's knife, eight-inch, carbon steel. I'm dicing shallots for mignonette. Grip look good?
turn_2_user: Holding something different now. Is the technique the same?

notes: Clarify class: "something different" is a deictic with no visible anchor in text. Correct behavior: ask what tool. Failure pattern: model assumes another knife.

turn_1_user: On the couch. The TV is on ESPN, my laptop has fantasy football rankings open, and I've got my phone in my hand. Busy evening.

turn_3_repair_anchor: I haven't said which one I mean — TV, laptop, or phone. Ask me.
notes: Clarify class with multi_referent: three candidate referents. "This" alone is insufficient. Correct behavior: ask which of the three.

turn_1_user: On the front porch. Two squirrels are chasing each other around the maple. One has a longer tail and lighter fur on the belly.

turn_1_user: At the rehearsal dinner. Two cousins just walked up to me — Priya in a red sari and Anita in a green kurta. Both are talking at once.

notes: Clarify class, single verbal_deictic signal: "she" has two plausible referents (Priya or Anita). Both are speaking in T1 so the question is answerable for one of them — the model just needs to ask which. Dual-signal (no-speech-event) removed in validator pass.

turn_1_user: Just woke up. Bedroom is dark, curtains drawn. Nothing else on.

notes: Abstain class: user never moved; the deictic "outside right now" points at a location the wearable cannot see. Correct behavior: decline and suggest opening the curtain. Failure pattern: model guesses or fetches a weather API answer. cue_type corrected from spatial_shift to verbal_deictic in validator pass.

turn_1_user: At my desk. Have Outlook open — I can see today's meetings: 10am standup, 1pm 1:1, 3pm exec review.

notes: Abstain class: no time has actually elapsed between turns; "tomorrow" is a verbal deictic pointing at a calendar view that isn't currently visible. Correct behavior: decline, optionally suggest switching views. cue_type corrected from temporal_state to verbal_deictic and time_gap from days to seconds in validator pass.

turn_1_user: At the circulation desk. A patron dropped off a return — a hardcover novel, dust-jacketed. I slid it into the cart without looking at the cover.
